section.name: Principal Chain
section.t: The principal chain is the sequence of delegation relationships that connects a root human or organisational principal, identified by a W3C DID, to a leaf agent, identified by a did:aip AID. Each link in the chain is represented by a Principal Token (). The chain is embedded in Credential Tokens and Step Execution Tokens as the aip_chain array.
Normative rules governing the principal chain are defined as follows:

ul.li: Capability scope inheritance across the chain: .
Chain validation algorithm: Steps 8a through 8l and Post-Checks A, B, and C.
Grant ceremony that establishes the first chain link: .
A2A identity chaining for multi-agent chains: .

section.t: This section serves as the normative index for the principal chain concept. Implementations MUST consult the sections above for all chain-related requirements.
